acceptance.assertion: runtime-drift-check against a manifest-valid canonical-runtime-snapshot does not emit checkout-not-git as a blocker solely because the immutable snapshot intentionally has no .git metadata.
Explicit writable or checkout-bound Registry roots continue to report unavailable Git identity, stale HEAD, origin/main drift and dirty paths fail-closed.
The canonical exception is allowed only when runtime identity proves a valid immutable release, valid canonical Registry inventory/tree digest and matching source commit.
Tests cover canonical snapshot, malformed snapshot identity, explicit non-Git root and stale or dirty Git checkout cases.

goal: Make runtime-drift-check understand the immutable canonical Registry snapshot contract so a valid non-Git canonical read-only snapshot is not reported as a checkout-not-git blocker, while explicit writable or checkout-bound drift checks remain strict.

metadata.safety_boundary: Do not weaken drift detection for explicit CLI worktrees, mutable registries, stale checkouts, dirty checkouts or mismatched release/Registry identities.
metadata.source: Live post-refresh readback on 2026-07-22: immutable Bureau runtime and canonical Registry snapshot both matched commit 453a63326a3b69cc733da6f2454c5723001601f4, runtime-identity and check were valid, but runtime-drift-check returned status=blocked solely because the intentional canonical snapshot is not a Git worktree.

title: Harden runtime-drift-check for canonical read-only snapshots
